Bash(python3 << 'EOF'
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np

img = Image.open\('/Users/robertwoodley/code/lawoodley.github.io/public/tridents-keep.png'\).convert\('RGB'\)
arr = np.array\(img\)
print\(f"Image size: {img.size}, shape: {arr.shape}"\)

# Sample some pixels in the trident area to understand its color range
# Trident is roughly center-x, mid-to-lower y
h, w = arr.shape[:2]
print\(f"Center region sample pixels:"\)
for y in range\(h//4, 3*h//4, h//16\):
    for x in range\(w//3, 2*w//3, w//8\):
        r, g, b = arr[y, x]
        print\(f"  \({x},{y}\): RGB\({r},{g},{b}\)"\)
EOF)

from PIL import Image

arr = np.array\(img\)

print\("Horizontal scan at y=270 \(trident head\):"\)
for x in range\(80, 350, 10\):
    r, g, b = arr[270, x]
    lum = 0.299*r + 0.587*g + 0.114*b

print\("\\nHorizontal scan at y=380 \(shaft\):"\)

    r, g, b = arr[380, x]
    lum = 0.299*r + 0.587*g + 0.114*b
    print\(f"  x={x}: RGB\({r},{g},{b}\) lum={lum:.0f}"\)
EOF)

Bash(python3 << 'EOF'
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np

img = Image.open\('/Users/robertwoodley/code/lawoodley.github.io/public/tridents-keep.png'\).convert\('RGB'\)
arr = np.array\(img\)

# Check the trident tip area \(upper prongs ~y=230-260\)
print\("Upper prong area y=230-265 center:"\)
for y in range\(230, 266, 5\):
    for x in range\(190, 260, 10\):
        r, g, b = arr[y, x]
        lum = 0.299*r + 0.587*g + 0.114*b
        if lum > 80:
            print\(f"  \({x},{y}\): RGB\({r},{g},{b}\) lum={lum:.0f}  *** bright ***"\)

# Find bounding box of all dark pixels \(lum < 45\) in the trident region
print\("\\nDark pixel extents \(lum < 45\) in center region:"\)
region = arr[200:570, 140:300]
r_ch = region[:,:,0].astype\(float\)
g_ch = region[:,:,1].astype\(float\)
b_ch = region[:,:,2].astype\(float\)
lum = 0.299*r_ch + 0.587*g_ch + 0.114*b_ch
mask = lum < 42
ys, xs = np.where\(mask\)
if len\(ys\):
    print\(f"  Y range: {ys.min\(\)+200} to {ys.max\(\)+200}"\)
    print\(f"  X range: {xs.min\(\)+140} to {xs.max\(\)+140}"\)
EOF)

Bash(python3 << 'EOF'
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np

img = Image.open\('/Users/robertwoodley/code/lawoodley.github.io/public/tridents-keep.png'\).convert\('RGB'\)
arr = np.array\(img\)

# Crop to trident region with padding
pad = 20
y1, y2 = 200 - pad, 535 + pad
x1, x2 = 175 - pad, 295 + pad
region = arr[y1:y2, x1:x2].copy\(\)

r = region[:,:,0].astype\(float\)
g = region[:,:,1].astype\(float\)
b = region[:,:,2].astype\(float\)
lum = 0.299*r + 0.587*g + 0.114*b

# Trident mask: dark body OR bright silver highlights
trident_mask = \(lum < 44\) | \(lum > 95\)

# Dilate mask slightly using a simple max-pooling trick with numpy
from numpy.lib.stride_tricks import sliding_window_view
padded = np.pad\(trident_mask, 2, mode='constant', constant_values=False\)
dilated = np.zeros_like\(trident_mask\)
for dy in range\(5\):
    for dx in range\(5\):
        dilated |= padded[dy:dy+trident_mask.shape[0], dx:dx+trident_mask.shape[1]]

# Replace background \(non-trident\) with white
output = region.copy\(\)
output[~dilated] = [255, 255, 255]

# Tighten crop to where mask has content
rows = np.any\(dilated, axis=1\)
cols = np.any\(dilated, axis=0\)
rmin, rmax = np.where\(rows\)[0][[0, -1]]
cmin, cmax = np.where\(cols\)[0][[0, -1]]
tight = output[rmin:rmax+1, cmin:cmax+1]

out_path = '/Users/robertwoodley/code/lawoodley.github.io/public/trident.jpg'
Image.fromarray\(tight\).save\(out_path, 'JPEG', quality=95\)
print\(f"Saved: {tight.shape[1]}x{tight.shape[0]} px → {out_path}"\)
EOF)
